title: 10 best herbal teas for numbness in hands
intro_desc: Herbal teas for numbness in hands are a natural remedy that can provide relief from tingling, prickling, and numb sensations in the fingers and hands. These teas work by improving blood circulation, reducing inflammation, and relaxing the muscles to alleviate nerve compression, which often causes numbness. Examples of herbal teas that help with numbness in hands include ginger, peppermint, and ginkgo biloba. Drinking these teas regularly can significantly improve daily life by enabling individuals to perform tasks without discomfort or pain, such as typing, cooking, or even holding a simple cup of coffee.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with numbness in hands because it contains a unique blend of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and essential oils that work together to improve circulation and reduce nerve inflammation. The tea's active ingredients, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, have been shown to stimulate the body's natural healing processes, helping to alleviate numbness, tingling, and weakness in the hands. By promoting healthy blood flow and reducing oxidative stress, maidenhair tree teas can help restore sensation and function to affected areas, providing relief from numbness in the hands.
remedy_properties: Vitamin C content: Maidenhair tree tea contains high amounts of vitamin C, which helps to improve blood circulation and oxygen supply to the hands, reducing numbness caused by poor circulation.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains various anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation in the nerves and surrounding tissues, alleviating numbness and tingling sensations.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of maidenhair tree tea help protect nerve cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to numbness and other neuropathic symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to reduce numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
Barks: The bark of the maidenhair tree has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to relieve numbness and discomfort in the hands, as well as promote blood flow to the affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried ginkgo biloba leaves measuring one teaspoon per eight ounces of water.
Rinse the ginkgo biloba leaves with filtered water to remove any impurities and debris.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in boiling water for five to seven minutes allowing the active compounds to release.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup removing the solids and leaves.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consumption and enjoy up to three cups daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper teas helps with numbness in hands because of its rich antioxidant properties, which help to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, work to dilate blood vessels, allowing for increased oxygen and nutrient flow to the hands. This enhanced circulation helps to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations, promoting overall hand health and function. Additionally, bell pepper teas' natural analgesic properties help to soothe and calm irritated nerve endings, further contributing to a reduction in numbness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots are known to contain analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving numbness and pain in the hands.
Barks: The barks of the bell pepper plant have been traditionally used to create teas that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the hands, which can contribute to numbness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried capsicum annuum fruit or purchase from a reputable source for 2-3 grams per serving.
Combine the dried capsicum annuum fruit with boiling water in a heat-resistant cup and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container to remove solids and residue.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey and 1/2 teaspoon of lemon juice to enhance flavor and solubility before serving warm.
Drink the tea thrice daily for at least 6 weeks to alleviate numbness in hands and improve overall circulation.
remedy_properties: Cayenne pepper tea: Contains capsaicin, which helps to increase blood flow and reduce inflammation, potentially relieving numbness in the hands.
Ginkgo biloba tea: Has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help improve circulation and alleviate numbness caused by poor blood supply or nerve damage.
Valerian root tea: Possesses sedative and analgesic properties, which can help reduce numbness-related discomfort, anxiety, and stress.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree teas helps with numbness in hands because it has been traditionally used to relieve symptoms of PMS, including tingling sensations in the extremities. The tea's active ingredient, casticin, has been shown to have a positive effect on hormonal imbalances and inflammation, which can contribute to numbness in the hands. Additionally, chaste tree teas contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and irritation in the nerves, allowing for improved circulation and sensation in the affected areas, providing relief from numbness and tingling sensations.
remedy_properties: Aldosterone regulation: Chasteberry tea helps regulate aldosterone, a hormone that plays a crucial role in maintaining healthy blood flow and reducing numbness in the hands by promoting proper circulation.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids and terpenoids present in chaste tree tea exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with numbness in the hands.

Neuroprotective compounds: Chasteberry tea contains neuroprotective compounds like casticin and actein, which may help protect against nerve damage and promote healthy neural function, potentially alleviating numbness in the hands.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in iridoid glycosides, which help to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by improving blood circulation.
Flowers: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce numbness and pain in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried vitex agnus-castus fruit per cup of boiling water.
Add the measured vitex agnus-castus fruit to a teapot and pour boiling water over it immediately.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming it within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with numbness in hands because of its unique properties that stimulate blood circulation and nerve function. The tea's active compounds, such as anethole and limonene, help to increase oxygenation and nutrients delivery to the peripheral nerves, relieving compression and constriction that can cause numbness. Additionally, fennel tea's anti-inflammatory effects reduce swelling and irritation around the nerves, promoting a healthy environment for proper nerve function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the nerves and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in fennel tea help protect nerve cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to numbness and other symptoms of neuropathy.
Muscle relaxant properties: Fennel tea's muscle relaxant properties can help relieve tension and spasms in the hands and fingers, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them an effective remedy to relieve numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a unique compound called anethole, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe numbing pain and discomfort in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried fennel seeds and place it in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Boil one cup of pure water in a kettle and pour it over the fennel seeds.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the herbal compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer and discard the solids.
Drink the tea hot or warm immediately, consuming one cup two to three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mountain arnica teas helps with numbness in hands because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and discomfort. The tea's active ingredients, such as helenin and flavonoids, work to relax tense muscles and improve blood flow, which can help alleviate tingling and numbness sensations caused by conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome or nerve damage. Regular consumption of mountain arnica tea may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate numbness in the hands.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds: Arnica tea contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, helping to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Improves circulation and blood flow: The active compounds in arnica tea help to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, which can increase oxygen and nutrient delivery to affected areas, reducing numbness and improving overall hand function.

Nerve-protective and antioxidant properties: Arnica tea contains antioxidants that protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in nerve tissues, helping to reduce the likelihood of nerve damage or degeneration that can cause numbness and tingling in the hands.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote blood circulation, making them effective for relieving numbness in hands.
Rhizomes: Containing volatile oils that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, rhizomes can help to soothe numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried arnica montana flowers and boiling water for a soothing tea infusion.
Measure one teaspoon of dried arnica montana flowers per cup of water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea leaves using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove loose particles and solids.
Combine the infused liquid with an equal amount of honey to enhance flavor and sweetness, and let it cool.
Pour the cooled arnica montana tea into a cup and enjoy three times daily for optimal numbing relief effects within 30 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe teas helps with numbness in hands because its natural anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and ease pressure on nerves, thus alleviating numbness. The tea's antioxidant-rich compounds also improve blood circulation, which enables oxygen and nutrients to reach the nerve endings more efficiently. Additionally, aloe's ability to relax muscles and calm the nervous system helps reduce muscle tension, further contributing to relief from hand numbness. By promoting overall circulatory health and reducing inflammation, herbal aloe teas can help restore feeling and sensation in numb hands.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected areas, which can contribute to numbness in the hands.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of aloe vera tea help to neutralize free radicals that can damage nerve tissue and contribute to numbness or tingling sensations in the hands.

Vasodilatory effects: Aloe vera's vasodilatory effects, which are attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic compounds, can help to improve blood flow and reduce constriction of blood vessels, which may help to alleviate numbness in the hands by increasing oxygen and nutrient delivery to affected areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and magnesium, which help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in hands.
Rhyzomes: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and discomfort associated with numbness in hands.
remedy_recipe: Measure out two tablespoons of dried aloe vera leaves for every cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured aloe vera leaves with one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and one teaspoon of dried st. john's wort flowers.
Pour the boiling water over the herbal mixture and let it steep for 10-15 minutes to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool for five minutes before drinking, taking two cups per day as needed to alleviate numbness in hands.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with numbness in hands because its essential oils, particularly menthol, increase blood flow to the extremities. This enhances oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the nerve endings, reducing numbness and tingling sensations. The calming and soothing properties of peppermint also help to relax the muscles and reduce muscle spasms that can contribute to numbness. As a natural stimulant, peppermint tea can also improve circulation and reduce inflammation, further alleviating numbness in the hands.
remedy_properties: Analgesic properties: Peppermint tea contains compounds like menthol and menthone, which have analgesic properties that help reduce pain and numbness in the hands by blocking pain receptors.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in peppermint tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nerves and tissues of the hands, thereby alleviating numbness.

vasodilatory properties: Peppermint tea's menthol content causes blood vessels to dilate, improving circulation and increasing oxygenation to the hands, which can help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations caused by poor circulation or nerve compression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant are used due to their rich concentration of menthol, which helps to stimulate blood flow and relieve numbness in the hands.
Bark: The bark of the peppermint plant contains a high amount of calcium, magnesium, and potassium, which can help relax muscles and reduce numbness in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or purchase dried peppermint herb from a health food store.
Measure two teaspoons of dried peppermint herb or four teaspoons of fresh peppermint leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured peppermint herb with boiling water and let it steep for five to seven minutes in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate heat-resistant cup, discarding the solids if desired.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature before consuming it to alleviate numbness in your hands within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with numbness in hands because it promotes blood circulation, which can help to reduce tingling sensations. The tea's active compounds, such as valerenic acids and isovaleric acid, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, calming down overactive nerve endings that may be contributing to numbness. Additionally, valerian root has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate pressure on nerves and promote healing of damaged tissues, leading to improved nerve function and reduced numbness in the hands.
remedy_properties: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid, a key component of valerian root, has been shown to reduce numbness and tingling sensations by improving blood circulation and nerve function, allowing for better transmission of signals from the brain to the hands.

GABA-mimetic effect: Valerian tea contains compounds that mimic the action of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate muscle tone and relaxation. This GABA-mimetic effect can help reduce numbness and tingling sensations by calming down overactive nerves.

Antioxidant properties: Valerian tea is rich in antioxidants, which help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. By reducing inflammation and free radical damage, valerian tea may help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, which is responsible for their sedative and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving numbness in hands.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the valerian plant has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and numbness in the hands, as well as promote blood flow to affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and dry them thoroughly for at least two weeks to preserve their potency.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried valeriana officinalis root for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Bring the measured water to a boil in a teapot or kettle, then reduce heat and simmer for five minutes to release the active compounds.
Steep the valeriana officinalis root in the hot water for an additional seven minutes to allow the flavors and nutrients to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool before drinking, allowing the body to absorb the valerenic acid and its beneficial effects on numbness in hands.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort teas helps with numbness in hands because of its natural ability to promote blood flow and reduce inflammation. The tea contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to improve circulation and increase oxygenation to the extremities, helping to alleviate tingling and numbness sensations. Additionally, St John's wort's anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe compressed nerves and restore normal functioning in the hands, providing relief from numbness and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antidepressant compounds: St John's Wort tea contains a range of antidepressant compounds, including hyperforin and hypericin, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote blood flow to the extremities, potentially alleviating numbness in the hands.

Analgesic properties: The active ingredients in St John's Wort tea have analgesic (pain-relieving) properties, which may help to reduce or eliminate numbness in the hands by reducing nerve compression and improving circulation.

Anti-inflammatory effects: St John's Wort tea has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which can help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the hands, potentially relieving numbness caused by conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome or peripheral neuropathy.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in relieving numbness and pain in the hands.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids and glycosides, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the affected area, reducing numbness and discomfort in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried hypericum perforatum flowers or 3-4 tablespoons of fresh flowers from a trusted source.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the herbs in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to dissolve fully.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Drink the tea immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen teas helps with numbness in hands because it contains methyl salicylate, a natural analgesic that penetrates deep into tissues to relieve pain and discomfort. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce swelling and stiffness in the hands, which can contribute to numbness. Additionally, wintergreen's warming sensation helps increase blood flow to the extremities, promoting healthy circulation and reducing feelings of numbness and tingling. As a result, drinking herbal wintergreen teas may provide welcome relief from numbness and discomfort in the hands.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Wintergreen tea contains high levels of menthol, which acts as a counterirritant and numbs the skin, providing relief from numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Analgesic properties: The tea's analgesic compounds, including methyl salicylate and camphor, help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with numbness, allowing for increased sensitivity and mobility in the affected areas.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Wintergreen tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the hands, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations, promoting a more normal range of motion and sensation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce numbness and discomfort in the hands.

Rhyzomes: Wintergreen rhyzomes contain high concentrations of wintergreen oil, which has analgesic and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing numbness and promoting circulation in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems for optimal potency and flavor.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by rinsing it gently under cold running water.
Combine one teaspoon of dried gaultheria procumbens leaves with eight ounces of boiling water to steep for seven minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool and congeal before refrigerating it for up to three days.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat numbness in hands with herbal teas is by drinking a blend of ginger, turmeric, and peppermint teas. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain, while turmeric contains curcumin, which improves blood circulation and reduces numbness. Peppermint tea relaxes the muscles and helps alleviate tension, promoting overall hand health. Drink 2-3 cups daily to experience relief from numbness in your hands.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to numbness in hands that are treated with herbal teas are conditions such as arthritis, carpal tunnel syndrome, and peripheral neuropathy. Herbal teas like turmeric tea, ginger tea, and ginkgo biloba tea have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the joints and hands. Additionally, herbs like St. John's Wort and lavender have been used to alleviate nerve-related numbness and tingling sensations. These teas may be consumed on their own or in combination with other natural remedies.
